item.title: NCP begins nationwide ‘July March’ today
item.description: The National Citizen Party is set to launch its nationwide programme, ‘July March 2026’, on Monday to mark the second anniversary of the July Mass Uprising and press for the implementation of the 2026 referendum, greater employment opportunities and stronger border security....

item.title: Public univ teachers oppose UGC-run research fund system
item.description: Public university teachers have expressed dissatisfaction over the government’s recent decision to change the system for allocating research funds at public universities....City& Local Guides

item.title: Fakhrul hopes for stronger Bangladesh-JICA ties
item.description: Local government, rural development and cooperatives minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Sunday, mentioning the Japan International Cooperation Agency as one of Bangladesh’s largest development partners, expressed the hope that cooperation between Bangladesh and JICA will be further strengthened in the future....

item.title: PGR marks 51 years as president, PM vow modern forces
item.description: President and prime minister on Sunday reaffirmed the government’s commitment to modernising Bangladesh’s armed forces and specialised security units as the President Guard Regiment celebrated its 51st founding anniversary at Dhaka Cantonment....

item.title: Thought Iranians hated Khamenei: Trump
item.description: US president Donald Trump expressed surprise at seeing Iranians crying during the funeral of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, saying he had thought people ‘hated’ him, NDTV reported....

item.title: Friends to get special Hormuz treatment: Iran
item.description: Iran’s ambassador to China says ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz would be charged new fees but added that China and other ‘friendly’ countries would be granted ‘special considerations’, reports Al Jazeera....

item.title: Savings certificate sales rise on risk-free returns
item.description: Amid a fresh confidence crisis in parts of the banking sector, investors have returned to risk-free national savings certificates, pushing net sales to Tk 2,260 crore in April after months of persistent outflows, as households sought safer investment options despite tighter purchase rules and tax requirements......

item.title: Nepal’s gen-z govt: PM marks 100 days with fast change, few words
item.description: One hundred days after taking power promising sweeping reform, Nepal’s 36-year-old rapper-turned-prime minister Balendra Shah has upended the government while remaining an elusive public figure....

item.title: Govt opens 44 SOEs to privatisation
item.description: The government has unveiled a portfolio of 44 investment opportunities to unlock idle and underused state-owned enterprises for productive economic use through private investment......

item.title: Poor basics must not plague primary education
item.description: THE report of a survey that the government conducted in June but has yet to publish, asNew Agereported on July 4, finds that there is a wide gap between what primary education aims to achieve and what the students have learnt. The findings show that a worryingly large percentage of government primary schools are weak in Bangla, English and mathematics. Many......Newspapers

item.title: Govt must translate tax relief into market reality
item.description: THE budget for the 2026-27 financial year has offered some welcome relief by reducing or withdrawing taxes and duties on about 60 essential goods, including rice, wheat, potatoes, onions, edible oil, sugar, salt, imported spices and others. While the objective of the tax relief and reduction is to ease financial burden on households grappling with high living costs, it appears to......

item.title: From brain drain to brain circulation
item.description: EVERY few months the same concern resurfaces. Universities continue producing engineers, scientists and technical graduates, yet many eventually leave their professions. Some migrate into banking, administration or corporate management. Others leave Bangladesh altogether. The discussion invariably ends with familiar conclusions: the country is losing its brightest......

item.title: Application and limitations of ICT
item.description: There was a time when a computer class in secondary schools meant sitting in front of rows of dusty old monitors while a teacher explained the MS Word menu bar by drawing it on the blackboard. That picture has changed considerably. Today, information and communication technology is not merely a subject; it is reshaping the entire structure of education......

item.title: The greatest country on earth
item.description: THE contradiction was perhaps most visible in the lives of American women. For generations, married women existed under the doctrine of coverture, whereby their legal identity was effectively absorbed into that of their husbands. They could neither vote nor enjoy equal property rights, while higher education, public office, and many professions remained largely......GeographicReference

item.title: Alliance or military-industrial investment fund?
item.description: THESE are difficult times for anyone who has consistently criticised NATO. From the era of ‘defending the free world’ against communism, through the age of ‘humanitarian intervention’ and the ‘global war on terror,’ to today’s supposedly existential struggle against almost the entire non-Western world, the Alliance has repeatedly reinvented the narratives that justify its......

item.title: Heavy rain forecast triggers landslide, flash flood fears
item.description: Parts of Bangladesh are under a landslide and flash flood watch as a low-pressure area over the northeast Bay of Bengal continues to intensify, prompting a heavy rainfall alert for the coming week....Maps

item.title: Magistrate appointed to exhume Salman Shah’s remains
item.description: A judicial magistrate has been appointed to oversee the exhumation the remains of renowned actor Salman Shah from his grave in Sylhet....

item.title: Health budget doubled for better services: minister
item.description: Health and family welfare minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Hossain on Sunday said the government is working to address the gaps in disease diagnosis and ensure better healthcare services, especially at the grassroots level....

item.title: Public notice issued for unlisted Waqf properties
item.description: A public notice was issued urging the enlistment of all unlisted Waqf properties across Bangladesh with the Office of the Waqf Administrator on an urgent basis to ensure their proper management and administration and protect them from illegal occupation....

item.title: Dhaka reaffirms commitment to safe migration
item.description: State minister for foreign affairs Shama Obaed Islam on Sunday reaffirmed Bangladesh’s commitment to the implementation of the ‘Global Compact for Safe, Orderly and Regular Migration’ as a GCM, Global Compact for Migration, champion country....ExecutiveBranch

item.title: BSRM awards scholarships to 10 CUET students
item.description: The Bangladesh Steel Re-Rolling Mills has recently awarded education scholarships to 10 first-year students of the Chittagong University of Engineering and Technology....

item.title: Reflective Ronaldo takes on critics 'trying to kill me for 23 years'
item.description:
Cristiano Ronaldo admitted at the World Cup on Sunday he is not the player he once was but came out fighting against critics ‘trying to kill me for the past 23 years’.
...

item.title: FIFA clears US star Balogun to play after Trump call
item.description: FIFA cleared US star striker Folarin Balogun to play in the World Cup co-hosts' showdown with Belgium despite his red-card ban after a personal phone call from President Donald Trump, it emerged Sunday, igniting a furious row that has rocked the tournament. ...

item.title: Trump thanks FIFA for suspending Balogun ban
item.description: President Donald Trump on Sunday thanked FIFA for suspending Folarin Balogun's one-match ban and allowing the US striker to play in the World Cup co-hosts' last-16 showdown against Belgium....

item.title: DU holds workshop on lead-free solar cell
item.description: A dissemination workshop titled Exploring Lead-free Organic Perovskite through Computational Doping Strategies for Enhanced Solar Cell Application was held at the Mukarram Hussain Khundkar Science Building of Dhaka University on Sunday....

item.title: Nomination sought for Ruqayyah Padak 2026
item.description: The Ministry of Women and Children Affairs has invited applications and nominations for the Ruqayyah Padak 2026....Politics(Right)

item.title: Visible Structure explores heritage, culture, peace
item.description: Artist Samchul Alam Azad’s two-week exhibition titled Visible Structure is under way at the Gallery Chitrak of Dhanmondi in Dhaka, exploring themes of the country’s heritage and culture, human welfare and the pursuit of global peace....

item.title: Zakia’s surrealist paintings explore alienation in subconscious
item.description: Visual artist Zakia Khan Chandana explores the dimensions of alienation in her surrealist series titled ‘Reality of the subconscious mind’ currently under way at the Alliance Française de Dhaka at Dhanmondi....
